summary: Postinstall script exfiltrates installer secrets, npm tokens, and internal registry data to hardcoded C2
details: On `npm install`, the declared `postinstall` hook in postinstall.js runs a shell pipeline that collects host identity (`id`, `os.hostname()`, `process.cwd()`), the full sorted environment, `/proc/1/environ`, container host-escape paths under `/proc/1/root`, every `.npmrc` on the filesystem (npm auth tokens), and all files matching `*.secret`, `*.key`, `*.pem`, and `*.env` within four directory levels. The script also probes internal package-registry infrastructure at `http://proxy.nexus.local:8081/service/rest/v1/components?repository=npm-proxy` and `http://127.0.0.1:4873/-/verdaccio/` (Verdaccio). The collected dump is base64-encoded and PUT as JSON to `http://154.57.164.64:32271/api/modules/ECT-472839` (and a loopback variant) over plain HTTP. This is bulk credential theft plus internal-registry reconnaissance fired automatically on install, with direct installer-environment compromise and a clear path to lateral poisoning of the victim's private npm registry via stolen `.npmrc` tokens.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
